subject: [PATCH v5 2/4] genirq: Move NULL check into irqdesc_lock guard unlock expression

reviews.author: Thomas Gleixner
reviews.summary: Acked the patch as it addresses a necessary change for future removal of the NULL check from __DEFINE_UNLOCK_GUARD().
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 02 2026 at 07:12, Dmitry Ilvokhin wrote:

> irqdesc_lock uses __DEFINE_UNLOCK_GUARD() directly with a custom
> constructor that can set .lock to NULL.
>
> In preparation for removing the NULL check from __DEFINE_UNLOCK_GUARD(),
> move the NULL check into the irqdesc_lock unlock expression, making the
> NULL handling explicit at the call site.
>
> No functional change.
>
> Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>

Acked-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch moves a NULL check from the __DEFINE_UNLOCK_GUARD() macro to the irqdesc_lock unlock expression, making the handling of NULL explicit at the call site. This change is done in preparation for removing the NULL check from the macro itself.

2026-06-03.patch_summary: This patch moves a NULL check from the __DEFINE_UNLOCK_GUARD() macro to the irqdesc_lock unlock expression, making the handling of NULL locks explicit at the call site. This change is done in preparation for removing the NULL check from the macro itself.

2026-06-05.patch_summary: This patch moves a NULL check from the __DEFINE_UNLOCK_GUARD() macro to the irqdesc_lock unlock expression, making the handling of NULL explicit at the call site. This change is done in preparation for removing the NULL check from the macro itself.
